world.title: Use of toxic Pfas in consumer goods must be urgently restricted, MPs say
world.description: Commons committee heard from residents of Yorkshire town with the highest levels of ‘forever chemicals’ in UK

world.title: Ethiopia’s climate action steps begin yielding results as meteorological experts meet in Addis Ababa
world.description: Ethiopia says its deliberate and concrete efforts to combat climate change are beginning to produce visible results, with major investments in renewable energy, environmental protection, and nationwide conservation initiatives helping the country strengthen its climate resilience. This was revealed by the Ethiopian Meteorological Institute during its first-ever scientific conference being held in Addis Ababa in […]

world.title: New pact aims to shield civilians in DRC conflict
world.description: After years of brutal conflict and broken promises, the Democratic Republic of Congo and the M23 armed group have reached a limited but meaningful agreement

world.title: Pilot’s selfie led to mid-air collision in F-15K fighter jet, says South Korea’s air force
world.description: One pilot ordered to repay some of the $600,000 of damage caused by collision in 2021

world.title: The Enduring Legacy of Saint George in Greece
world.description: George is one of the most popular names in Greece and Saint George is one of the most beloved saints throughout the Christian world.

world.title: China woman missing for 28 years found buried in mall's flower bed; suspect allegedly underwent plastic surgery
world.description: Renovators got far more than they bargained for when their works dug up woman's skeletal remains and a couple's dark past.The body of a woman in China who went missing for 28 years has been found in a flowerbed of a shopping mall in Sichuan, Beijing News reported on Monday (April 20).Wu Yanping's remains were completely skeletal, suggesting that she...

world.title: ‘When you lose your main selling point, not much remains’ - Britain’s PM Keir Starmer on the brink
world.description: Britain’s prime minister Keir Starmer came to power on a promise to govern efficiently after years of political turmoil.

world.title: DSWD releases P3.2-M aid to OFWs hit by Middle East crisis
world.description: DSWD has released over P3.2 million in financial aid to repatriated OFWs affected by the Middle East crisis, providing cash assistance, psychosocial support, livelihood aid, and medical help to thousands of returning Filipino workers as part of the government’s unified reintegration program.

business.title: Second London Tube strike to begin at midday
business.description: RMT members are walking out in a dispute over proposed changes to their working week.

business.title: Shippers to save GH¢800m as container charges are capped, GUTA welcomes move
business.description: Importers and exporters in Ghana are set to benefit from significant cost savings following a directive by the Ghana Shippers’ Authority to cap the Container Administrative Charge at GH¢550 per twenty-foot equivalent unit (TEU).

business.title: AI-driven Hong Kong stock inflows from mainland China slow as investor options multiply
business.description: Wave of listings provides more ‘pure-play’ AI investment options across mainland and Hong Kong exchanges, BNP Paribas says.

business.title: 10 smallcap stocks jump up to 99% in April alone! Do you own any?
business.description: India’s smallcap space is witnessing a sharp rebound, with several stocks delivering outsized gains of up to 99% in April. The Nifty Smallcap 250 has surged about 15% so far this month, clearly outperforming the Nifty 50, which continues to face pressure from sustained foreign institutional selling. Here’s a look at the top 10 performers for the month. Smart Rebound

business.title: SIA deepens role at Air India amid record losses
business.description: Singapore Airlines has moved some employees into key roles at Air India across flight operations, engineering and maintenance in recent months.

business.title: Participation in EMA’s demand response scheme grows as firms curb peak electricity use
business.description: The scheme aims to ease pressure on the grid and stabilise prices, says the Energy Market Authority, as energy-intensive businesses turn to battery storage and solar to manage rising electricity costs.

business.title: Petrol, diesel prices may rise Rs 25-28 per litre starting next week after Bengal, Tamil Nadu elections: Kotak Institutional Equities
business.description: Petrol Diesel Price Hike: Fuel prices in India may rise significantly after April 29. Global crude oil costs are high, causing losses for Indian refiners. Kotak Institutional Equities predicts a potential increase of Rs 25-28 per litre. This could push petrol prices to unprecedented levels. Higher fuel costs will impact transportation and everyday expenses for consumers across the nation.

science.title: Calcium In Immune Cells May Trigger Anxiety Behaviours, Study Suggests
science.description: A study in mice links calcium signalling in microglia, the brain's immune cells, to anxiety and obsessive compulsive behaviours, offering new treatment insights.

science.title: Astronaut takes photo of Cranfield University from the ISS
science.description: Jack Hathaway tells Cranfield University he had to wait for a break in the British weather.

science.title: Cane toads upend the theory that changes in evolution are glacial
science.description: Invasive cane toads in Japan are growing bigger and changing more rapidly than suggested by long-held theories about the pace of evolution, scientists say in a new study.

science.title: New AI Method Captures Long-Range Atomic Interactions in Complex Molecules
science.description: Researchers from Google DeepMind in Berlin, BIFOLD, and the Technical University of Berlin have introduced a new machine learning method that enables global atomic interactions in chemical systems ...

science.title: 289-million-year-old mummified reptile reveals how breathing began on land
science.description: A remarkably preserved, mummified reptile from 289 million years ago is rewriting what we know about how animals first breathed on land. This tiny creature, Captorhinus aguti, reveals the earliest known version of the rib-powered breathing system used by modern reptiles, birds, and mammals — a crucial innovation that helped vertebrates thrive outside water.

science.title: Sleeping cancer cells hijack the wound healing pathway to wake up
science.description: New research shows that the awakening of disseminated cancer cells is caused by inflammation in the tissue microenvironment.

science.title: Switching neural code may solve ongoing face-recognition debate
science.description: Face patch cells in macaque monkeys initially respond to images of any object but rapidly transition to attend to faces exclusively, a new study finds.

ai.title: US Stock Market: IBM shares slide after software weakness clouds Q1 results
ai.description: IBM shares fell in after-hours trading after its Q1 results signalled a slowdown in revenue growth, particularly in its software division. While earnings beat estimates, concerns around AI-driven disruption and softer segment performance weighed on sentiment. Strong infrastructure growth and enterprise demand provided some support to the overall outlook.

ai.title: Samay Raina’s Still Alive becomes most-watched comedy special with 53.7 million views - here are top 7 comedians dominating the internet
ai.description: Samay Raina’s stand-up special Still Alive has gone viral, reportedly crossing 53.78 million views, 3.8 million likes, and 2 lakh comments on YouTube. Uploaded on April 7, it’s being called one of the fastest-growing comedy specials globally. The buzz also grew after an AI chatbot mention went viral online.

ai.title: India's app market is booming - but global platforms are capturing most of the gains
ai.description: Non-gaming apps, led by streaming and AI, are driving growth, even as India's spending per user lags global peers.

ai.title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for Clinicians, offers free access to healthcare professionals
ai.description: OpenAI has introduced ChatGPT for Clinicians, a specialised version designed to ease documentation, research, and clinical workflows. With free access for verified US healthcare professionals, the move reflects growing reliance on AI in medicine, while signalling a broader push towards safer, more efficient, and scalable digital healthcare systems worldwide.

ai.title: SoftBank seeks $10 billion margin loan backed by OpenAI shares: Bloomberg
ai.description: SoftBank is reportedly seeking a massive $10 billion loan, leveraging its stake in ChatGPT's creator, OpenAI. This move signals the Japanese tech giant's intensified commitment to artificial intelligence, following a period of significant financial volatility. The loan, potentially extendable, follows a substantial $40 billion bridge loan secured earlier this year to fuel further AI investments.
